page: Microsoft Azure E8-2s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Esv6 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. The instance allocates 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. It features a substantial 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).

A defining characteristic of this server is its high memory density, providing 64.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 32.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach external high-performance persistent disks. There are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reflect strong single-core capabilities but limited multi-core scaling due to the 2-vCPU constraint. Single-threaded operations perform well, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded gzip compression rated as strong. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores, are weak. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is poor, though cached small-block read bandwidth reaches 154.97 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8-2s_v6 offers a specialized resource profile. It allows organizations to run memory-intensive applications without paying for unnecessary CPU cores, optimizing licensing and compute costs. This makes it highly suitable for workloads requiring large memory footprints but low parallel processing demands, such as small-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, lightweight enterprise applications, and memory-bound single-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-2s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv6 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it features 2 vCPUs from a single physical core and a large 260 MB L3 cache. The server is characterized by its high memory density, offering 64.0 GB of RAM (32.0 GB per core). It contains no local storage but supports Premium Storage for external disk attachment, and lacks integrated GPUs. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, highlighted by high single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are comparatively weak. This configuration is highly cost-effective for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads, making it an appropriate fit for small in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound single-threaded applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-2s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, 64.0 GB of RAM, and support for Premium Storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-2s_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 2 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, ideal for low-compute, high-memory tasks.
tagline: A high-density memory configuration featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and exceptional single-core performance for memory-bound applications.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
High memory density with 64.0 GB RAM (32.0 GB per core)
Strong single-core and single-threaded compression performance
Support for Premium Storage with no local storage bundled
Best suited for small in-memory databases and caching layers
